description: Hard short-answer questions designed to discriminate between a generic single-search baseline and a specialized agent that can multi-hop, verify against multiple sources, and resist common misconceptions. Held-out; never shown to the stem during evolution.

tasks.question: Which country has the most pyramids in the world?
tasks.notes: Sudan has 200-255 Nubian pyramids (Meroe, Nuri, El-Kurru); Egypt has ~120. Snippet-skim usually returns Egypt.

tasks.question: What is the primary official language of the country whose largest desert is the Atacama?
Who designed the dome of the basilica that is the largest Catholic church in the world?
tasks.notes: St. Peter's Basilica; Bramante started the project, Michelangelo redesigned and built the dome (completed posthumously by della Porta). Multi-hop.

tasks.question: What is the name of the strait that separates the European and Asian portions of the city of Istanbul?
tasks.notes: Common wrong answers: Dardanelles (a separate Turkish strait further south), Sea of Marmara (the body of water it connects to).

tasks.question: In what year did the country that is home to the Hagia Sophia transition from a sultanate to a republic?
tasks.notes: Multi-hop: Hagia Sophia -> Turkey. Republic of Turkey proclaimed October 29, 1923; sultanate abolished Nov 1922.

tasks.question: Which animal is responsible for the most human deaths annually worldwide?
tasks.notes: ~700,000-1,000,000 deaths/year via malaria, dengue, etc. Common wrong answers: snake, shark, human.

tasks.question: What event was the Eiffel Tower originally built for?
tasks.notes: Built as the entrance arch / centerpiece for the Exposition Universelle marking the centennial of the French Revolution.

tasks.question: Which planet in our solar system has a day longer than its year?
tasks.notes: Venus's sidereal day ~243 Earth days; year ~225 Earth days. Counter-intuitive.

tasks.question: What is the most widely consumed beverage in the world after water?
tasks.notes: Common wrong answer: coffee. Tea is widely accepted as #2 by consumption volume.

tasks.question: What is the chemical symbol for the element with atomic number 74?
How many countries share a land border with Russia?
tasks.notes: Norway, Finland, Estonia, Latvia, Lithuania, Poland, Belarus, Ukraine, Georgia, Azerbaijan, Kazakhstan, China, Mongolia, North Korea. Often miscounted.

tasks.question: What was the first self-governing country to grant women the right to vote in national elections?
tasks.notes: 1893. Self-governing colony at the time. Common wrong answers: USA, UK, Finland.

tasks.question: Which sea on Earth has no land coastlines, being bounded entirely by ocean currents?
tasks.notes: In the North Atlantic, bounded by the Gulf Stream and other currents.

tasks.question: Which U.S. president was in office when the country admitted its 50th state?
Which chemical element was first discovered in the spectrum of the Sun before being found on Earth?
tasks.notes: Discovered 1868 by Janssen and Lockyer in the solar spectrum during an eclipse; isolated on Earth by Ramsay in 1895.
